item_name: Argentina: currency controls and pharma import restrictions (2016-2025)
access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (currency controls fundamentally affect ability of all formal procurement channels - PAMI, obras sociales, BNDE, private - to import oncology drugs and APIs); historical: out_of_pocket when imports stalled.
procurement_channels: All formal pharmaceutical importers had to pass through successive currency control regimes: SIMI/DJAI (pre-2016), liberalization 2016-2019, CEPO reinstated Sept 2019, SIRA introduced Oct 2023, replaced by SEDI Dec 27 2023, SEDI eliminated by ARCA RG 5651/2025 effective Feb 26 2025, main cepo cambiario lifted Apr 14 2025. Pharmaceutical imports were given relatively favorable treatment (30-day payment cycle vs. 180 days for general goods) under Milei reforms.
key_actors: BCRA (Banco Central de la Republica Argentina) - sets foreign exchange access rules; AFIP / ARCA (Agencia de Recaudacion y Control Aduanero, post-Oct 2024 successor to AFIP) - administered SIRA/SEDI authorization; Secretaria de Comercio - approval authority; Ministerio de Economia / Hacienda; Aduana / customs; CILFA, CAEME, CAPGEN (industry chambers - lobbying for pharma exceptions); ANMAT (technical import authorization for medicines); Ministerio de Salud (sectoral coordinator).
formulary_coverage: Indirect impact: cisplatin and doxorubicin are largely produced locally / regionally and less FX-sensitive; carboplatin partly imported; trastuzumab originator is fully imported (USD-priced); biosimilar trastuzumab from India/Korea also FX-sensitive. Currency controls historically delayed trastuzumab originator imports.
judicial_access_volume: Indirect link: currency-driven shortages and price hikes of imported oncology drugs were a leading driver of amparo de salud filings, particularly 2018-2019 (post-2018 currency crisis) and 2023-2024 (post-Dec 2023 devaluation). [uncertain: quantitative attribution]
registration_timeline_generic: Not applicable - currency controls run parallel to ANMAT registration.
registration_authority: Not applicable. BCRA, AFIP/ARCA, Secretaria de Comercio collectively administer currency/import controls.
biosimilar_pathway: Not applicable to currency controls per se; but currency restrictions historically incentivized local biosimilar manufacturing (locally produced biosimilars require less FX). Decree 1741/2025 promoting local biosimilar production explicitly cites import substitution rationale.
reform_status: enacted: full sequence - CEPO reimposed Sept 2019 (Macri exit measures, M. Pesce administration BCRA); CEPO tightened 2020-2023 (Cristina Kirchner / Massa era); SIRA created Oct 2023 (Massa, Resolution AFIP 5466/23) replacing SIMI; DNU 70/2023 (Dec 21 2023) liberalization framework; SEDI created via General Resolution AFIP 5466/2023 modified Dec 27 2023 - removed import licensing requirement; ARCA RG 5651/2025 eliminated SEDI effective Feb 26 2025; BCRA Communication A 8226 (Apr 14 2025) lifted main cepo cambiario for individuals and most corporate operations; partial restrictions remained for select capital movements but progressively eased through Sept 2025 (Infobae Sept 19 2025 - cepo lifted for foreign-employed dollar earners).
currency_mechanism: Phased timeline: (1) 2016-Aug 2019 - liberalized FX under Macri / Sturzenegger; (2) Sept 1 2019 - CEPO 1.0 reimposed (Macri last days, BCRA Communication A 6770); (3) 2020-2023 - CEPO 2.0 progressively tightened, multiple parallel exchange rates (oficial, blue, MEP, CCL, solidario), 30-35% PAIS tax on FX purchases; (4) Oct 2023 - SIRA system layered on top, requiring multi-agency approval including Tax Authority, Customs, Secretaria de Comercio - severely blocked imports; (5) Dec 27 2023 - SEDI replaces SIRA under Milei, automatic statistical declaration only; pharmaceutical imports prioritized 30-day FX payment schedule; (6) Apr 14 2025 - main cepo lifted, individuals can buy unlimited USD; (7) Sept 2025 - further cepo removal for specific worker categories.
pharma_import_cost_impact: Severe and well documented. Pre-2016 base: imports impeded by DJAI. 2018: peso lost ~50% vs. USD; 2019 inflation 54%, peso further devalued. 2020-2023 CEPO 2.0 created brecha cambiaria of 100%+ between official and parallel rates - importers using official rate at advantage but rationed; 2023 inflation >200%; Dec 2023 one-off 54% devaluation collapsed peso budgets of payers (PAMI, obras sociales) for USD-priced biologics; 2024 inflation averaged 178% YoY. Cancer drugs in Argentina priced 123% above UK average for high-priced agents (ASCO Global Oncology 2018). Argentine public spending on high-cost medicines surged from ~AR$204 million (2018) to AR$4.5 billion (2022) reflecting both volume and price/FX inflation. Pharmaceutical imports given priority status - 30-day payment cycle vs. 180 days for general goods - mitigates but does not eliminate FX shock.
financial_mechanism: Indirect: currency controls determine the effective peso cost of imported drugs and APIs; payers (PAMI, obras sociales, BNDE) absorb FX shock through budget overruns or reduced volumes. SUR/SURGE caps lag FX moves.
payer_debt_crisis: Currency shocks (2018, 2023) systematically triggered payer debt accumulation - PAMI's AR$400B 2025 pharmacy debt and obras sociales arrears traceable in part to FX-driven cost surges that outpaced budget transfers.
health_budget_pct_gdp: Argentina ~9.5% GDP (2022, total); imports ~30-40% of pharmaceutical market by value [uncertain: precise current share].
shortage_rate: No unified shortage rate published; CEPO years (2020-2023) anecdotally produced periodic stockouts of imported APIs and finished oncology drugs. [uncertain: quantitative shortage rate]
shortage_causes: Currency controls + brecha cambiaria + import licensing (SIRA) collectively delayed FX access and halted/postponed imports of biologics. Rolled into broader 'cepo cambiario' shortage narrative. SIRA Oct-Dec 2023 was particularly disruptive as it stalled US$ debt to foreign suppliers (~US$50bn commercial debt at peak).
shortage_monitoring_system: No FX-shortage-specific monitoring; ANMAT pharmacovigilance and trade chambers (CILFA, CAEME) self-reported; industry press (Pharmabiz, El Cronista) tracked.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: 2018-2019 currency crisis caused trastuzumab and other biologic price hikes and intermittent stockouts at obras sociales (anecdotal). 2020-2021 COVID-era SIRA precursor (SIMI A and B) delayed imports. Oct-Dec 2023 SIRA freeze - significant supplier non-payment reported. Dec 2023 50%+ devaluation triggered price renegotiations and temporary shortages of imported oncology biologics. Cisplatin/carboplatin global shortage 2023-2024 layered on top. [uncertain: specific dated events]
source_citation: 1) Trade.gov - Argentina Eliminates Capital Controls and Payment Timelines (2025); 2) DLA Piper - Argentina reforms the economic legal landscape (Jan 2024); 3) Mayer Brown - Argentina passes sweeping economic reforms (Jul 2024); 4) Beccar Varela - Report on DNU 70/2023; 5) Allende & Brea - Decree 70/2023 - Basis for the Reconstruction; 6) Wikipedia - Decree 70/2023 and Economic reforms of Javier Milei; 7) Aerodoc - The Milei Era: New Customs Regulation in Argentina; 8) Chequeado - Cronologia del cepo cambiario (2011-2025); 9) Cato Institute - Milei's Key Pending Task: Ending Argentina's Currency Controls; 10) BCRA Communication A 6770 (Sept 2019), Communication A 8226 (Apr 2025); 11) ARCA General Resolution 5651/2025 - SEDI elimination; 12) Infobae (Sept 19 2025) - Quitan el cepo cambiario; 13) Simon-Kucher - How Hyperinflation Affects P&MA Strategy of Innovative Drugs (Argentina); 14) Statista - Pharmaceutical products inflation rate in Argentina; 15) ASCO Global Oncology 2018 - Cancer Drug Prices in Argentina and UK Comparison.
source_type_granular: government_primary (BCRA, ARCA, Boletin Oficial) + legal_regulatory_tracker (DLA Piper, Beccar Varela, Mayer Brown) + investigative_news (Infobae, Buenos Aires Times) + industry_association_FIFARMA (Simon-Kucher, FIFARMA WAIT)
data_reliability: high for legal/regulatory chronology; medium for quantitative pharma-specific FX impact
uncertain: quantitative attribution of amparo to FX shock
precise current import share of pharmaceutical market
quantitative shortage rate during CEPO years
